title: Inulin Clearance
buzzwords: exogenous filtration marker requiring IV infusion
small, neutral, biologically inert tracer
freely filtered with no secretion or reabsorption
not produced or metabolized by the body
needs timed urine collections and specialized assay
impractical clinically, reserved for research
plant-derived fructose polymer
gold-standard reference for GFR measurement

title: Creatinine Clearance
buzzwords: endogenous marker produced at near-constant rate
byproduct of muscle creatine phosphate metabolism
production scales with muscle mass, age, sex, diet
cheap blood test with reliable assays
freely filtered with minimal protein binding
rises 24-48 hours after acute kidney injury
10-20% tubular secretion overestimates true GFR
blocked by cimetidine and trimethoprim

title: Cystatin C
buzzwords: alternative endogenous filtration marker
produced at constant rate by all nucleated cells
independent of muscle mass and diet
improves accuracy in sarcopenic or elderly patients
often combined with creatinine for best estimate
low-molecular-weight protein freely filtered
akin to beta-2-microglobulin and beta-trace protein
13 kDa cysteine protease inhibitor

title: eGFR Estimation from Serum Creatinine
buzzwords: clinical surrogate calculated from a blood draw
indexed to body surface area
hyperbolic, non-linear relationship to its input marker
insensitive at near-normal kidney function
incorporates age, sex, and body size
reported in mL/min/1.73m^2
serum value doubles for each halving of function
creatinine-based estimating equation

title: KDIGO CKD Staging
buzzwords: framework stratifying chronic kidney function
two-dimensional grid combining filtration and proteinuria
albuminuria worsens prognosis at any given stage
hyperkalemia and metabolic acidosis emerge in mid stages
salt-sensitive volume overload at advanced stages
dialysis or transplant required at the lowest tier
thresholds at 60 and 15 mL/min/1.73m^2
Kidney Disease: Improving Global Outcomes classification

title: Hemodynamic Determinants of GFR
buzzwords: balance of Starling forces across glomerular capillary
hydrostatic pressure opposed by rising oncotic pressure
filtration fraction set by arteriolar tone
mesangial contraction lowers filtration surface area
NSAIDs constrict the upstream arteriole
ACE inhibitors dilate the downstream arteriole
hypoalbuminemia raises filtration, lowers filtration fraction
efferent constriction raises GFR while lowering RBF
